toolchainSource.description: Toolchain download source for this SDK.

enumDescriptions: %configuration.sifli.buildWithSaveCheck.enum.prompt%
%configuration.sifli.buildWithSaveCheck.enum.saveAll%
%configuration.sifli.buildWithSaveCheck.enum.saveCurrent%
%configuration.sifli.buildWithSaveCheck.enum.doNotSave%

server.description: Optionally connect to an existing `probe-rs dap-server` session on IP and Port, e.g. '127.0.0.1:50000'

logFile.description: The path to the log file. This option is ignored if either the `server` or `runtimeArgs` is specified. If no location is specified, the behaviour depends on `--log-to-folder

logToFolder.description: Enable logging to the default folder. This option is ignored if either the `logFile` or `runtimeArgs` is specified.`

consoleLogLevel.description: The level of log info printed to the console. This does NOT affect the RUST_LOG defined in the `env` property.
enumDescriptions: The console will only contain error messages and process status messages.
The console log will also contain high level information about interactions between the extension and the debug adapter.
The console log will also contain detailed information about interactions between the extension and the debug adapter.

runtimeExecutable.description: An OS resolvable path to the Probe-rs debugger executable.

runtimeArgs.description: String array of arguments to provide the startup arguments for the Probe-rs debugger executable.

env.description: Environment variables defined as a key value pair. The 'key' is the name of the environment variable, and the 'value' is value of the environment variable.

cwd.description: The working directory of the debugger, typically the RUST crate root

probe.description: Use this flag to select a specific probe in the list. Use '--probe VID:PID' or '--probe VID:PID:Serial' if you have more than one probe with the same VID:PID.

chip.description: Please specify the appropriate chip from the list of supported chips reported by running `probe-rs chip list`.

chipDescriptionPath.description: Use this option to add custom target chips from a file.

connectUnderReset.description: This option will result in the target reset pin being held high during the attach operation.

speed.description: Specify the protocol speed in kHz.

wireProtocol.description: The correct wire protocol to use.
enumDescriptions: Use the Serial Wire Debug (SWD) protocol.
Use the Joint Test Action Group (JTAG) protocol.

allowEraseAll.description: Allow the session to erase all memory of the chip or reset it to factory default.

flashingConfig.description: These options are applied when flashing one or more `program_binary` files to the target memory.

haltAfterReset.description: Halt all cores on the target after reset.

verifyBeforeFlashing.description: Verify chip contents before erasing, to prevent unnecessary reprogramming.

fullChipErase.description: Do a full chip erase, versus page-by-page erase.

restoreUnwrittenBytes.description: Restore erased bytes that will not be rewritten from ELF.

binaryFormat.description: One of the supported binary formats probe-rs uses for flashing the target binary.
enumDescriptions: The target binary file contains the verbatim contents of the flash.
The target binary file conforms with the [Intel HEX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel_HEX) format.
The target binary file conforms with the [ELF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Executable_and_Linkable_Format) format.
The target binary file conforms with the [ESP-IDF bootloader](https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/latest/esp32/api-reference/system/app_image_format.html#app-image-structures) format

baseAddress.description: The address in memory where the binary will be flashed to.

skip.description: The number of bytes to skip at the start of the binary file.

idf_bootloader.description: The path (relative to `cwd` or absolute) to the ESP-IDF bootloader.

idf_partition_table.description: The path (relative to `cwd` or absolute) to the ESP-IDF partition table.

coreConfigs.description: Each MCU core has a mandatory `programBinary` as well as several other optional properties.

coreIndex.description: The zero based index of the MCU core for this session

programBinary.description: The path (relative to `cwd` or absolute) to the binary for your target firmware

svdFile.description: The path (relative to `cwd` or absolute) to the CMSIS-SVD file for your target core

rttEnabled.description: If true, the debugger will open an RTT Terminal tab for each of the active channels on the target.

rttChannelFormats.description: RTT channel configuration. Unlisted active channels will be configured with `dataFormat=String', and 'showTimestamps=true'.

channelNumber.description: The channel number to which this data format applies.

dataFormat.description: One of the supported data formats for RTT channels.
enumDescriptions: String (text) format.
Binary Little Endian format.
defmt (see: https://defmt.ferrous-systems.com).
The target will add data to the channel only if it fits completely, otherwise it will skip the data.
The target will add as much data to the channel as possible, without blocking.
The target will block until there is enough space in the channel to add the data.

showTimestamps.description: Enable the inclusion of timestamps in the RTT output for `dataFormat=String`.

showLocation.description: Enable the inclusion of defmt location information in the RTT output for `dataFormat=Defmt`.

logFormat.description: The default format string to use for decoding defmt logs.

catchReset.description: If true and supported by the target, the debugger will stop when the target resets.

catchHardfault.description: If true and supported by the target, the debugger will stop when the target generates a hard fault.

configurationSnippets.label: SiFli probe-rs Debug: Launch
configurationSnippets.description: A new configuration for embedded debugging of a user selected rust file with probe-rs.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: yarn run build:webview && yarn run compile && yarn run copy-templates
scripts.pretest: yarn run compile && yarn run lint
